hypothesis: Combining L1 soft-distance reward loss with SupCon hand loss achieves full fidelity on both axes
key_finding: Scale imbalance: L_hand ~5.26 vs L_reward ~0.002 at convergence; hand SupCon dominates ~99.96% of gradient signal, crowding out reward metric structure. Hand accuracy improved to 67.8% (new best) but reward Spearman rho dropped to 0.083 (worst of all variants).
next_steps: v4: Separate representation into disjoint subspaces (4 dims reward, 4 dims hand) to prevent gradient interference
v4 alt: Explicit loss re-scaling to equalize gradient magnitudes before combining
Investigate: normalize L_reward to match SupCon scale before backprop
